patterns.description: Intel Killer Wi-Fi 7 BE200NGW (service: Netwaw16) fails PDC power-down callback during Modern Standby, blocking PCIe RP DRIPS entry. Causes instant CS exit (<100ms) + SCM 7021/7003 service failures on wake.
symptoms: Sleep Study Session Duration < 100ms with ExitReason='Transition To Sleep'
SCM Event ID 7021 (Netwaw16 start timeout) on wake
SCM Event ID 7003 (Netwaw16 dependency failure) on wake
PCIe RP at _SB.PC02.RP03 appears in TopBlockers at >1% active time
patterns.recommendation: Update Killer Wi-Fi 7 BE200NGW driver to latest via Intel Driver & Support Assistant. Disable Rivet Networks background services (KNDBWMService, KillerAnalyticsService, KillerNetworkService) if not required. Verify PCIe ASPM (CLx L1.1/L1.2) enabled in BIOS for WiFi root port. Disable Remote Desktop if not needed (RemoteDesktopEnabled=true prevents WiFi D3-Cold). Check AP GTK rotation interval (increase to >=3600s to reduce WPA3 reauthentication frequency).

patterns.description: Antivirus filter driver causing I/O delays
patterns.recommendation: Antivirus filter drivers are a common source of boot delays and I/O latency. Check if the AV product has an optimized boot mode or consider updating to the latest version.
OEM preloaded services can significantly delay boot. Consider disabling auto-start for non-essential OEM services or setting them to delayed start.

patterns.description: Network driver with high DPC activity
patterns.recommendation: Network drivers with high DPC counts can cause audio glitching and UI stuttering. Update the network driver or check interrupt moderation settings.

patterns.description: Storage driver contributing to high flush counts
patterns.recommendation: Excessive disk flushing during boot increases boot time. Check if the storage driver firmware needs updating or if write caching can be enabled.

patterns.description: Excessive registry hive flushing during boot
patterns.recommendation: Registry hive flush storms during boot indicate poorly timed registry operations. Applications should defer registry writes until after the boot critical path.
SysMain (Superfetch) activity during boot is normal and helps with future boot performance. Only disable if it's causing excessive disk I/O on slow storage.
Windows Update operations during boot/login significantly increase boot time. This is typically temporary after receiving updates.
GPU driver initialization time affects time-to-desktop. Ensure the latest GPU driver is installed. For Intel iGPU, check GOP (Graphics Output Protocol) BIOS settings.
Services taking >5 seconds to start should be reviewed. Consider converting from Automatic to Automatic (Delayed Start) or Demand for non-critical services.
High NTFS metadata I/O can indicate a fragmented or very full disk. Check disk fragmentation levels and ensure at least 15% free space.

patterns.description: Unknown PDC activator V1_018 with infinite timeout blocking CS exit
patterns.recommendation: An unidentified PDC activator (V1_018 'V1_Unknown') with infinite timeout (0xFFFFFFFF) can block Connected Standby exit. Use 'powercfg /sleepstudy' and kernel debugger '!podev' to identify the owning driver. Common on Lenovo ThinkPad with Intel Alder Lake.

patterns.description: LID open wake causes PCIe Root Port D-state stall on Alder Lake
patterns.recommendation: LID open wake via ACPI _Q51 triggers the full PDC/CS exit path (unlike power button which bypasses PDC). Intel PCIe Root Port DEV_64A0 (Alder Lake) can stall during D0 entry on LID wake. Update BIOS/EC firmware and Intel chipset driver. Test with EnablePowerButtonSuppression=0.

patterns.description: Power button suppression enabled during Modern Standby
patterns.recommendation: EnablePowerButtonSuppression=1 suppresses power button events during Connected Standby. If LID wake is failing, the user cannot recover via power button. Set to 0 for debugging: reg add HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power /v EnablePowerButtonSuppression /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
